title: snprintf truncation unchecked in lichen_ipv6_addr_to_str
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.c:189-199

snprintf() return value is checked for negative (encoding error) but NOT for truncation. When ret >= buflen, output is truncated but function returns success (0).

What goes wrong: If buflen shrinks or format changes, truncated addresses would be logged/used without error. The pre-check (buflen < LICHEN_IPV6_ADDR_STR_LEN) mitigates for well-behaved callers, but function contract should be robust.

Fix: Add check for (size_t)ret >= buflen and return -ENOSPC.
